name: United Planning Organization - Petey Greene Community Svc. Center
description_short: United Planning Organization (UPO) is a DC-based nonprofit providing 60+ years of free financial counseling, tax preparation, job training, and affordable housing advocacy to low-income residents.
description_long:
United Planning Organization has served Washington DC residents for over 60 years as a comprehensive community development nonprofit. Originally established to address poverty and economic inequality, UPO has evolved into a multi-service organization with deep roots in the DC community and significant advocacy influence on local policy.

UPO offers free and low-cost services across four primary domains: (1) Early childhood education through Early Head Start and early learning programs; (2) Youth development via CREATE and POWER programs serving grades 1-12 with academic enrichment, mentorship, and college preparation; (3) Workforce development including job training, job placement, job readiness coaching, and national certification pathways; and (4) Financial stability services including tax preparation, affordable housing advocacy, community health initiatives, and financial counseling. The organization explicitly markets itself as helping DC residents "reach economic security."

UPO distinguishes itself through its integrated approach—addressing education, employment, and financial stability holistically rather than in isolation. The organization has demonstrated measurable impact: $1.4 million awarded in scholarships since 1990, 859 children served in Early Head Start, and 245 customers earning national certifications. UPO also serves as a policy advocate, publishing research on eviction trends, TANF programs, and affordable housing crises affecting DC low-income families. Leadership (President/CEO Andrea Thomas) actively voices positions on policy issues like childcare pay equity.

As a legitimate 501(c)(3) nonprofit, UPO offers genuine free counseling rather than predatory financial products. However, the website provides limited specifics about service eligibility, wait times, or geographic coverage within DC. The organization appears to focus primarily on youth and working-age adults; elderly services are mentioned only through the "Foster Grandparents" volunteer mentorship program. For those outside DC or seeking specialized credit repair, UPO may not be the right fit.
pros: Established 60+ year track record with deep DC community roots and demonstrated impact metrics
Comprehensive integrated services (youth education, job training, financial counseling, housing advocacy) under one organization
Free tax preparation and financial counseling services with no predatory products or high-cost loans
Active policy advocacy publishing research on DC eviction trends, TANF benefits, and affordable housing
College scholarship program ($10,000 per student, 5 scholarships annually) plus workforce certification support
Hands-on mentorship programs (CREATE, POWER, Foster Grandparents) addressing opportunity gaps for underserved students
Non-profit structure aligned with consumer benefit rather than profit extraction
cons: Website lacks clear information on service eligibility criteria, geographic coverage areas within DC, or application processes
No published wait times or service capacity data despite apparent high demand
Limited information about specific financial counseling offerings, debt management tools, or credit-related services
Primarily DC-focused; services appear unavailable to residents outside Washington DC
Website emphasizes youth/workforce programs more than adult financial counseling or credit repair services
best_for: DC residents seeking free tax preparation and financial counseling without predatory products
Low-income DC families with school-age children needing educational enrichment and college preparation
DC job seekers requiring workforce training, job readiness coaching, or career pathway development
DC renters facing housing instability seeking affordable housing resources and advocacy support
diagnosis: UPO is best for DC residents seeking legitimate free financial counseling, tax prep, and integrated economic stability services without predatory lending. The main caveat is geographic limitation to Washington DC and unclear service eligibility/availability for all residents.
services: Free tax preparation and filing assistance
Financial counseling and economic security guidance
Early Head Start and early childhood education programs
Workforce job training and certification pathways
Job placement and job readiness coaching
Policy advocacy and research on DC poverty/housing issues
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 2907 Martin Luther King Jr. Ave., Se, , Washington, DC 20032
